methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $78M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Vivodyne is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Andrei Georgescu and Dan Huh, having raised $78M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private frontier-tech peers in biotech and health—including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B, Seed), and Neko Health ($1.8B, Series B)—along with 3 public sector comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $78M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yields $156M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as Vivodyne's revenue is not disclosed. These two methods triangulate to a median valuation of $728M, with a defensible range of $133M (low) to $1.5B (high). The wide dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity within the peer set and the early maturity of Vivodyne relative to later-stage comparables. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, IP defensibility, and the sector's sensitivity to capital availability.
